name: Data Register — Object Array with Label/Value Mapping
summary: Populate a dropdown from an object-array data register using options.label and options.value to map display text and stored value
whenToUse: Dropdown where the display label differs from the stored value (e.g. ministry name vs code)
Register data is an array of objects with named properties
When you need to store a code or ID but show a human-readable label
notes: The register data is an array of objects, e.g.: [{"ministryCode": "EDUC", "ministryName": "Education"}, {"ministryCode": "HLTH", "ministryName": "Health"}]
Use `options.label` to specify which object property to display in the dropdown (defaults to `"label"` if omitted)
Use `options.value` to specify which object property to store as the form value (defaults to `"value"` if omitted)
The mapping uses lodash `_.get()` so nested paths like `"details.name"` are supported
Set `enum: [""]` as a placeholder in the data schema — the actual values are loaded from the register at runtime
